title_hint: FastAPI Dependency Injection: The Right Way
description: A practical guide to FastAPI's dependency injection system — why it exists, how it works, and patterns for keeping your routes thin and your logic testable.
audience: Python developers building REST APIs with FastAPI
outline: What dependency injection actually solves
FastAPI's Depends() — the basics
Sharing DB connections and service objects

title_hint: SQLite in Production: When It's the Right Call
description: SQLite gets dismissed as a toy database, but for many production workloads it outperforms Postgres in simplicity, latency, and cost. Here's when to use it and how.
audience: Backend developers choosing a database for a small-to-medium web app
outline: The SQLite stigma — and why it's mostly wrong
Read-heavy workloads where SQLite excels
WAL mode: enabling concurrent reads and writes
Backup strategies: Litestream and .dump
When you actually need Postgres

title_hint: Python Async/Await: The Traps Nobody Warns You About
description: async/await looks simple until you hit blocking calls, event loop conflicts, or subtle concurrency bugs. This post maps the landmines so you can avoid them.
audience: Python developers new to async programming or debugging async bugs
tone: candid, technical, example-heavy with code snippets
outline: How the event loop actually works
Blocking the loop with sync I/O — the #1 mistake
asyncio.gather vs asyncio.TaskGroup
Running sync code in a thread pool
Testing async code with pytest-asyncio

title_hint: Docker Multi-Stage Builds for Lean Python Images
description: A production Python Docker image shouldn't weigh 1 GB. Multi-stage builds let you compile, install, and copy only what you need — here's how to do it with uv.
audience: Python developers containerising their apps for the first time or optimising existing images
tone: step-by-step tutorial, practical, includes full Dockerfile examples
outline: Why image size matters in production
The builder stage: installing deps with uv
The runtime stage: copy .venv, nothing else
Layer caching: ordering instructions for speed
Scanning for vulnerabilities with docker scout

title_hint: uv: The Python Package Manager That Changes Everything
description: uv replaces pip, pip-tools, virtualenv, and pyenv in a single Rust binary that runs 10-100x faster. Here's what it does differently and why it matters.
audience: Python developers frustrated with slow installs, complex tooling, or dependency hell
tone: enthusiastic but measured, benchmark-driven, practical migration tips
outline: The fragmented Python packaging landscape
uv's resolver: why it's so fast
uv sync, uv add, uv run — daily workflow

Migrating from pip + requirements.txt

title_hint: HTMX Patterns That Replace 80% of Your JavaScript
description: HTMX lets you build dynamic UIs with almost no JS by putting hypermedia back in control. These five patterns cover the majority of real-world interactivity needs.
audience: Backend developers who want richer UIs without learning a JS framework
outline: Inline form submission and server-rendered feedback
Infinite scroll and load-more pagination
Live search with hx-trigger and debounce
Optimistic UI with hx-swap and CSS transitions

title_hint: Structured Outputs with Anthropic Tool Use
description: The best way to get reliable JSON from Claude isn't prompt engineering — it's tool use. Here's how to define a tool schema and extract structured data every time.

outline: Why plain prompts fail for structured output

title_hint: Tailwind CSS Without a Build Step: CDN, Play CDN, and Trade-offs
